references.text: Barrett, RL, EH Roalson, K Ottewell, M Byrne, SP Govindwar, SR Yadav, AS Tamboli, & AR Gholave. 2017. Resolving generic boundaries in Indian–Australasian Cleomaceae: Circumscription of Areocleome, Arivela, and Corynandra as distinct genera. Systematic Botany 42: 694-708.
Iltis, HH & TS Cochrane. 2007. Studies in the Cleomaceae V: a new genus and ten new combinations for the Flora of North America. Novon 17: 447-451.
Jacobs, M. 1960. Capparidaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 6: 61-105
Patchell, MJ, EH Roalson & JC Hall. 2014. Resolved phylogeny of Cleomaceae based on all three genomes. Taxon 62: 315-328

Su, J-X, W Wang, L-B Zhang & Z-D Chen. 2012. Phylogenetic placement of two enigmatic genera, Borthwickia and Stixis, based on molecular and pollen data, and the description of a new family of Brassicales, Borthwickiaceae. Taxon 61: 601-611

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 28 July 2024

species.citation: (L.) Raf. , Sylva Tellur. (1838) 110; ≡ Corynandra viscosa (L.) Cochrane & Iltis , Novon 23 (2014) 24; ≡ Cleome viscosa L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 672 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 103; Raganas et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 1194; ≡ Polanisia viscosa (L.) DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 1 (1824) 242 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 216. = Polanisia icosandra (L.) Wight & Arn. , Prodr. Fl. Ind. Orient. (1834) 22 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 209; ≡ Cleome icosandra L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 672. = Cleome acutifolia Elmer , LPB 7 (1915) 2574; Type: Elmer 12697 (US, lecto; B, BISH, BM, CAS, F, MO, NY, Z, isolecto), Palawan: Brooks Point (Addison Peak), Feb-1911.
species.distribution: ?Australia. BUGSUK (photos), LUZON: Cagayan (photos), MINDANAO: Davao del Norte, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Possibly native to Australia (Barrett et al. 2017). Nearly pantropical. Assumed to be non-native to the Philippines. A very common naturalized weed along roadsides, wastelands, rice fields, etc. at sea level and low and medium elevation.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 2 May 2022

species.citation: (Jacq.) Iltis , Novon 17 (2007) 448; ≡ Cleome serrata Jacq. , Enum. Syst. Pl. (1760) 26.
species.distribution: C & S America, Caribbean. MINDANAO: Surigao del Norte (photos).
species.notes: Widespread in waste, open places and in gardens (M.G.Q. Rule, pers. comm., 10-Nov-20).

species.citation: (Raf.) Iltis , Novon 17 (2007) 448; ≡ Cleome speciosa Raf. , Fl. Ludov. (1817) 86 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 101; ≡ Gynandropsis speciosa (Kunth) DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 1 (1824) 238, nom. illeg. ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 209; ≡ Cleome speciosa Kunth , Nov. Gen. Sp. (H.B.K.), quarto Ed. 5 (1821) 84, nom. illeg., non Raf.
species.distribution: Neotropics. LEYTE, LUZON: Cavite, La Union, NCR.
species.notes: Cultivated as an ornamental in SE Asia. Occasionally escaping.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 5 September 2022

species.citation: (L.) Briq. , Annuaire Conserv. Jard. Bot. Genève 17 (1914) 382 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 209; ≡ Cleome gynandra L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 671 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 101. = Gynandropsis pentaphylla (L.) DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 1 (1824) 238 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 216; ≡ Cleome pentaphylla L. , Sp. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1763) 938.
species.distribution: Africa, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Indian Ocean, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madagascar, Malay Peninsula, Middle East, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Ilocos Sur, NCR, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Davao, Davao del Sur, Zamboanga, MINDORO, TAGUBANHAN.
species.notes: Widely introduced in the Neotropics. A common roadside and rice field weed. 0-500m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 15 November 2019

species.citation: (DC.) Roalson & J.C.Hall , Syst. Bot. 42 (2017) 938 ; Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 987; ≡ Cleome rutidosperma DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 1 (1824) 241 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 103.
species.notes: First recorded in Malesia around 1920 (in Manila around 1948). Now a very common lowland weed along roadsides, waste places, etc. Invasive species (CABI 2017). 0m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 21 October 2024

species.citation: (Jacq.) Raf. , Sylva Tellur. (1838) 111; ≡ Cleome spinosa Jacq. , Enum. Syst. Pl. (1760) 26 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 208; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 102.
species.distribution: Neotropics. LUZON: Benguet, MINDANAO: Misamis Oriental (photos).
species.notes: Occasionally cultivated in the tropical and temperate zones. In the Philippines thoroughly naturalized and very abundant along streams in Benguet. 800m (photos).
